$comment: Reference case for spot_cone_falloff (intensity = saturate((theta-outerCutoff)/(innerCutoff-outerCutoff))). Two coefficients. The saturate() clamps to 0 outside the outer cone and 1 inside the inner cone — those clamped samples carry no gradient — so the sweep is centred on the transition band where both cutoffs are identifiable. Canonical values equal the library defaults (cos 15°/25°); the fit must hold the linear transition slope and offset.
notes: 40-point sweep over theta ∈ [0.88, 0.98] (cos of angle to spot axis). ≈24 samples land in the (outer, inner) transition band, which is what fixes the two cutoffs; absolute tolerance 0.01 (~0.6° of cone angle) is realistic given that the clamped tails contribute no gradient.
